question: Compare the NuScale general description of the CVCS from Ch.01 with the detailed chemistry specifications in Ch.05.

nuscale_ch01_0187.title: BTP 6-1, (March 2007): pH for Emergency Coolant Water for Pressurized Water ReactorsB.2 Spray Water pH and Water Chemistry Requirements for Fission Product Removal
nuscale_ch01_0187.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to certain regulatory criteria regarding pH and water chemistry for emergency coolant and fission product removal, as it does not use a containment spray system or sump but still collects ECCS water for recirculation. Some criteria, such as those related to minimum containment pressure and dual containment leakage paths, are not applicable to NuScale because its design lacks features like secondary containment and does not result in core uncovery during a LOCA. Overall, NuScale applies the intent of relevant criteria where applicable to its unique design.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet all relevant regulatory requirements, such as 10 CFR 50 and its appendices. These materials are designed, constructed, and tested to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance, with specific material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4.

nuscale_ch01_0366.title: DSRS 9.3.6, Rev 0: Containment Evacuation and Flooding SystemsII.1 GDC 2
nuscale_ch01_0366.summary: The section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for containment evacuation and flooding systems, specifically noting compliance with GDC 2 requirements as outlined in DSRS 9.3.6, Rev 0. It states that the NuScale design meets these criteria with no exceptions or additional comments.

nuscale_ch01_0363.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.7 Prevention of CVCS Holdup Tank Wall Buckling/Failure; CVCS Venting and Draining
nuscale_ch01_0363.summary: The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report states that the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) generally conforms to regulatory criteria, but only partially meets the requirements for prevention of holdup tank wall buckling/failure, as the NuScale design does not include holdup tanks subject to vacuum conditions referenced in the criteria. However, the NuScale CVCS will provide appropriate venting and draining capabilities as required. Other related criteria are either fully met or not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0749.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.3 Incidents of Moderate Frequency
nuscale_ch01_0749.summary: This section confirms that the NuScale reactor design conforms to the regulatory criteria for incidents of moderate frequency related to an inadvertent decrease in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system, as outlined in SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2. It notes that certain guidance is not applicable due to NuScale’s unique design, such as the absence of reactor coolant pumps, and that operator action is not required to mitigate such events.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across different power levels by establishing steady-state parameters for both primary and secondary systems, such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures. These parameters are calculated using thermal hydraulic software and verified by hand, with uncertainties and design margins considered to ensure efficient operation and safety. The design supports natural circulation with low pressure losses, and uncertainties related to steam generator tube plugging do not affect primary coolant flow rate determination.

nuscale_ch01_0750.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.4 Minimum Time Intervals for Required Operator Actions
nuscale_ch01_0750.summary: This section states that for the NuScale reactor design, operator action is not required to mitigate an inadvertent boron dilution event, so minimum time intervals for operator actions are not applicable. The NuScale design differs from traditional PWRs as it does not use reactor coolant pumps, and the relevant safety criteria are addressed through other means.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining pressure boundary integrity to prevent radionuclide release. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control systems, to ensure safe and efficient operation.

nuscale_ch01_0364.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.8 ITAAC
nuscale_ch01_0364.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS), including the Boron Recovery System. It confirms that the NuScale CVCS generally meets the applicable requirements, with partial conformance noted only for criteria related to holdup tank venting and draining, as the NuScale design does not include holdup tanks subject to vacuum conditions. Other referenced sections are either fully met or not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0361.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.5 Chemical and Volume Control System Design and Arrangement
nuscale_ch01_0361.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS), including boron recovery. It states that the NuScale CVCS generally conforms to the applicable DSRS requirements, with partial conformance noted only where specific criteria do not apply to the NuScale design, such as holdup tank requirements. The section also clarifies that certain SRP sections are not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0303.title: DSRS 9.1.3, Rev 0: Spent Fuel Pool Cooling and Cleanup SystemII.5 Specific Criteria to Meet GDC 63
nuscale_ch01_0303.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Spent Fuel Pool Cooling and Cleanup System’s conformance with regulatory criteria, specifically addressing compliance with General Design Criteria (GDC) 63. The system fully conforms to GDC 63 requirements, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0351.title: SRP 9.3.2, Rev 3: Process and Post-Accident Sampling SystemsII.2 Technical Specifications
nuscale_ch01_0351.summary: The section states that NuScale’s design generally conforms to the regulatory criteria for process and post-accident sampling systems, except for requirements that are not applicable to its design or are BWR-specific. Technical specifications for sampling systems are deemed not applicable due to prior NRC approval, while other aspects such as functional design, seismic classification, and environmental protection are confirmed as conforming or partially conforming, with specific notes on the chemical and volume control system’s safety functions.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, all designed in accordance with regulatory definitions and requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0439.title: SRP 10.4.6, Rev 3: Condensate Cleanup SystemII.2 Maintain indirect cycle PWR water quality to avoid corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (GDC 14)
nuscale_ch01_0439.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Condensate Cleanup System, specifically regarding maintaining indirect cycle PWR water quality to prevent corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (GDC 14). The NuScale design conforms to these requirements, noting that its steam generator configuration results in different secondary chemistry requirements compared to traditional designs.

nuscale_ch01_0359.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.3 Minimization of contamination
nuscale_ch01_0359.summary: This section summarizes NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) and related systems. It notes partial or full compliance with various safety and design requirements, highlighting that the primary CVCS safety function is to prevent inadvertent boron dilution, and that single-failure criteria apply specifically to safety-related isolation valves. Some criteria are not applicable due to differences in design or prior regulatory approvals.

nuscale_ch01_0301.title: DSRS 9.1.3, Rev 0: Spent Fuel Pool Cooling and Cleanup SystemII.3 Specific Criteria to Meet GDC 5
nuscale_ch01_0301.summary: Section II.3 of DSRS 9.1.3, Rev 0, states that the Spent Fuel Pool Cooling and Cleanup System fully conforms to the specific criteria required to meet General Design Criterion (GDC) 5, with no exceptions noted. This indicates that the system is designed to prevent sharing of structures, systems, and components among units in a way that would significantly impair safety functions.
